per_question.q: What index does pgvector use to speed up search and what is a typical number of lists?
per_question.answer: The ivfflat index speeds up search by clustering vectors into lists; a typical starting value is 100 lists. [1] It supports exact and approximate nearest-neighb

per_question.q: What similarity metric is most common for normalized text embeddings?
per_question.answer: Cosine similarity measures the angle between two vectors and is the most common metric for normalized text embeddings. [2] Vector databases store high-dimension

per_question.q: What overlap is recommended when chunking documents?
per_question.answer: Chunking splits documents into overlapping windows. [1] Overlap of roughly 10-20 percent of the chunk size preserves context across boundaries. [1] A good RAG s

per_question.q: What does faithfulness measure in a RAG system?
per_question.answer: A good RAG system abstains when retrieval confidence is low. [1] Faithfulness measures whether an answer is supported by the retrieved context. [1] Retrieval-au

per_question.q: How many dimensions does all-MiniLM-L6-v2 produce?
per_question.answer: The all-MiniLM-L6-v2 model produces 384-dimensional sentence embeddings and is a fast, strong baseline for semantic search. [1]

per_question.q: How many dimensions does Gemini text-embedding-004 produce?
per_question.answer: Gemini text-embedding-004 produces 768-dimensional embeddings and is accessed through the Google Generative AI API. [1] An embedding is a dense numeric represen

per_question.q: What does recall at k measure?
per_question.answer: Recall at k is the fraction of queries whose relevant document appears in the top k results. [1] Smaller chunks improve precision; larger chunks improve recall
HNSW is an alternative index that trades memory for very fast approximate search. [1]
